Enterprise Estates Nursing Center

CCN: 175475 · ENTERPRISE, KS 67441 · Dickinson County

Overview

Address
602 CRESTVIEW DRIVE, ENTERPRISE, KS 67441
Phone
7852638278
Certified beds
40
Avg daily residents
28 (69% of beds filled)
Ownership
Non-profit (other)
Provider type
Medicare and Medicaid
Medicare/Medicaid since
2004-08-15
Setting
Rural
1 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 1/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 1/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 3/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 1/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 1/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures — Data not available.

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 3.83 3.30 4.49 ≥ 3.48
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.46 0.58 0.54 ≥ 0.55 Below floor
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 0.89 0.73 1.04 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 2.48 1.99 2.90 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 1.35 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.00 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
3.43
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.41
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
4.02
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Staff turnover

Total nursing staff turnover
67.6%
annual
RN turnover
85.7%
annual
Administrators departed
1
last 12 months

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.17
higher acuity than avg
Case-Mix Index Ratio
0.85
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2025-11-17 11 11 8 80 1 80
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2024-01-17 14 10 4 339 1 339
Total weighted health score
144.8
lower is better

Deficiencies (36)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

1 A/B/C No actual harm — minor
32 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
3 J/K/L Immediate jeopardy
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. D 2025-11-17 2025-12-17
0726 Ensure that nurses and nurse aides have the appropriate competencies to care for every resident in a way that maximizes each resident's well being. D 2025-11-17 2025-12-17
0770 Provide timely, quality laboratory services/tests to meet the needs of residents. D 2025-11-17 2025-12-17
0605 Prevent the use of unnecessary psychotropic medications or use medications that may restrain a resident's ability to function. D 2025-11-17 2025-12-17
0628 Provide the required documentation or notification related to the resident's needs, appeal rights, or bed-hold policies. D 2025-11-17 2025-12-17
0657 Develop the complete care plan within 7 days of the comprehensive assessment; and prepared, reviewed, and revised by a team of health professionals. D 2025-11-17 2025-12-17
0700 Try different approaches before using a bed rail. If a bed rail is needed, the facility must (1) assess a resident for safety risk; (2) review these risks and benefits with the resident/representative; (3) get informed consent; and (4) Correctly install and maintain the bed rail. D 2025-11-17 2025-12-17
0730 Observe each nurse aide's job performance and give regular training. F 2025-11-17 2025-12-17
0756 Ensure a licensed pharmacist perform a monthly drug regimen review, including the medical chart, following irregularity reporting guidelines in developed policies and procedures. D 2025-11-17 2025-12-17
0868 Have the Quality Assessment and Assurance group have the required members and meet at least quarterly F 2025-11-17 2025-12-17
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. F 2025-11-17 2025-12-17
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2024-01-17 2024-02-16
0657 Develop the complete care plan within 7 days of the comprehensive assessment; and prepared, reviewed, and revised by a team of health professionals. D 2024-01-17 2024-02-16
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2024-01-17 2024-02-16
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. D 2024-01-17 2024-02-16
0699 Provide care or services that was trauma informed and/or culturally competent. D 2024-01-17 2024-02-16
0757 Ensure each resident’s drug regimen must be free from unnecessary drugs. D 2024-01-17 2024-02-16
0758 Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. D 2024-01-17 2024-02-16
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. E 2024-01-17 2024-02-16
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. F 2024-01-17 2024-02-16
0883 Develop and implement policies and procedures for flu and pneumonia vaccinations. E 2024-01-17 2024-02-16
0600 Protect each resident from all types of abuse such as physical, mental, sexual abuse, physical punishment, and neglect by anybody. J 2023-07-17 2023-08-14
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. J 2023-07-17 2023-08-14
0610 Respond appropriately to all alleged violations. K 2023-07-17 2023-08-14
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2023-04-03 2023-05-15
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. D 2022-06-07 2022-07-07
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2022-06-07 2022-07-07
0657 Develop the complete care plan within 7 days of the comprehensive assessment; and prepared, reviewed, and revised by a team of health professionals. D 2022-06-07 2022-07-07
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. E 2022-06-07 2022-07-07
0732 Post nurse staffing information every day. C 2022-06-07 2022-07-07
0756 Ensure a licensed pharmacist perform a monthly drug regimen review, including the medical chart, following irregularity reporting guidelines in developed policies and procedures. D 2022-06-07 2022-07-07
0757 Ensure each resident’s drug regimen must be free from unnecessary drugs. D 2022-06-07 2022-07-07
0758 Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. D 2022-06-07 2022-07-07
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. E 2022-06-07 2022-08-09
0881 Implement a program that monitors antibiotic use. F 2022-06-07 2022-07-07
0882 Designate a qualified infection preventionist to be responsible for the infection prevent and control program in the nursing home. F 2022-06-07 2022-07-07

Methodology & sources
  • Nursing Home Provider Info (nh-provider-info), vintage 2026, downloaded 2026-04-14 , 14,703 rows.
  • Nursing Home Penalties (nh-penalties), vintage 2026, downloaded 2026-04-14 , 16,915 rows.
  • Nursing Home Health Deficiencies (nh-deficiencies), vintage 2026, downloaded 2026-04-14 , 418,972 rows.
  • Nursing Home Ownership (nh-ownership), vintage 2026, downloaded 2026-04-14 , 160,393 rows.
  • Skilled Nursing Facility Cost Report (snf-cost-report), vintage 2023, downloaded 2026-04-14 , 14,120 rows.
